question: Is the in-model JIT-replay append-position baking caused by start_pos-dependent producers (RoPE), per the CUDA-graph-dynamic-scalar guidance?

isolated_diagnostics_all_ADVANCE.raw_src_append: positions 2048,2049,2050,2051 ALL written across TinyJit replays
isolated_diagnostics_all_ADVANCE.rope_like_src_append (k = rawk * freqs[s: ALL written -- a start_pos-dependent (rope-like captured-slice) append src ADVANCES correctly
isolated_diagnostics_all_ADVANCE.two_jit_prefill_then_fresh_decode_jit: ALL written -- the model's two-jit pattern does NOT bake
isolated_diagnostics_all_ADVANCE.canonical_store_prefill_then_opaque_appe: ALL written -- the canonical .after(store) prefill handoff does NOT corrupt the opaque-append decode

hypothesis_refuted: The proposed cause -- start_pos baked into the RoPE producer (freqs_cis[start_pos] slice) making the append receive stale K/V -- is REFUTED: an isolated append with a start_pos-dependent (rope-like) src advances correctly across replays. The append offset AND a position-dependent src both replay fine in isolation.
actual_cause: EMERGENT in the full 36-layer model forward; NOT reproduced by any isolated probe (rope src / two-jit / canonical-prefill / RESHAPE cache). The start_pos concretization that bakes the model's append happens somewhere in the full decode graph (36 layers + norm/lm_head/sampling), not in the append/rope producer alone.
implication: Building an opaque rope+append kernel (the proposed fix) would NOT address the model's baking, because the rope-dependent src is proven NOT to be the cause. The correct next step is to INSTRUMENT the model's captured decode graph (RUNTIME_KV on) to find which op concretizes start_pos for the append subtree -- e.g. dump the captured linear, check whether the append's 'start_pos' var is live in GraphRunner.vars/var_vals_replace or concretized, and bisect by disabling model components (lm_head/sampling/FFN) until the append advances.
note_on_observation: the model wrote ONLY the capture position (2050), i.e. the OFFSET baked (not just stale data) -- consistent with start_pos being concretized for the whole append node (offset + src share the 'start_pos' expr), but the concretizer is NOT the rope src (refuted).
